title: Realtime Collaborative Editor
subtitle: Google Docs-scale conflict resolution using CRDTs in the browser
description: A fully browser-based collaborative text editor built on Yjs CRDTs and WebRTC peer signaling. Supports concurrent multi-cursor editing with sub-50ms sync latency across LAN peers. Implements a custom undo/redo stack that respects concurrent edit history.

technicalChallenges.title: CRDT Merge Conflicts Under High Concurrency
technicalChallenges.description: Standard OT algorithms fail under simultaneous three-way edits. Yjs Y.Text with CRDT semantics resolves all merge cases deterministically without a central authority server, enabling true peer-to-peer convergence.

technicalChallenges.title: WebRTC Signaling Without a Dedicated Server
technicalChallenges.description: Used y-webrtc's built-in BroadcastChannel fallback for same-device tabs and a lightweight Cloudflare Worker as the ICE signaling relay, keeping infrastructure costs at zero for the demo tier.

assets.imageAlt: Screenshot of collaborative editor with two cursors visible in a shared document

title: Design System Engine
subtitle: Token-driven component library with automated visual regression testing
description: A monorepo-based design system built for a 40-engineer product org. Tokens are authored in YAML and compiled to CSS custom properties, iOS/Android primitives, and Figma variables simultaneously via a custom Style Dictionary transform pipeline.

technicalChallenges.title: Multi-Platform Token Compilation
technicalChallenges.description: Building a single YAML token schema that correctly transforms to platform-specific output required custom Style Dictionary transforms handling platform-specific naming conventions and value resolution.

technicalChallenges.title: Zero-Flake Visual Regression
technicalChallenges.description: Playwright screenshot comparisons produced 2–3% false-positive rates due to font rendering variance across CI OS images. Canvas-based comparison with configurable pixel tolerance eliminated flakes without masking real regressions.

assets.imageAlt: Component library documentation site showing button variants and design token table

title: WebGL Shader Lab
subtitle: Interactive GLSL shader editor with live preview and texture pipeline
description: A browser-based GLSL shader development environment with split-pane live preview, uniform inspector, and texture import. Built on raw WebGL2 with no three.js abstraction — every draw call is explicit. Used internally for rapid shader prototyping on a game UI project.

technicalChallenges.title: Zero-Latency Shader Recompilation
technicalChallenges.description: Debouncing recompilation to achieve sub-16ms perceived latency required syntax-error detection on every keystroke using a lightweight GLSL tokenizer, with full GPU recompilation deferred to 300ms after the last edit.

technicalChallenges.title: GPU Texture Memory Management
technicalChallenges.description: WebGL texture objects must be manually deleted to avoid GPU memory leaks. A reference-counted texture cache with LRU eviction tracks uniform sampler bindings and safely frees unreferenced textures across shader recompiles.

assets.imageAlt: Split-pane shader editor with GLSL code on the left and rendered output on the right

title: Accessibility Audit CLI
subtitle: Automated WCAG AA scanner with component-aware false-positive filtering
description: A Node.js CLI tool that wraps axe-core with project-specific configuration profiles to eliminate class-specific false positives — reducing audit noise by 60% without masking genuine violations. Integrates with CI pipelines as a blocking quality gate.

technicalChallenges.title: Context-Aware False Positive Filtering
technicalChallenges.description: axe-core flags decorative aria-hidden images as missing alt text when they appear inside labelled containers. A rule suppression layer correlates violation node ancestry against an allowed-context manifest to filter known-safe patterns.

assets.imageAlt: Terminal output showing accessibility audit results with violation counts and severity levels
